title: Crypto Hack Losses Drop Below $1B in First Half of 2026 Despite Record Attacks
summary: Crypto hacks hit a record 207 incidents in the first half of 2026, but total losses fell to $972 million, a significant drop from previous years. DeFi exploits, in particular, saw a 74% decrease in damage compared to 2022, according to Immunefi.
body:
The first half of 2026 saw a record number of crypto hacks, with 207 incidents reported, but the total financial losses from these attacks fell below $1 billion for the first time in years. According to Immunefi, the total losses amounted to $972 million, a notable decline from previous highs.

The decline in losses is largely attributed to a 74% drop in DeFi exploit damage compared to 2022. This suggests that security measures in the DeFi space have improved significantly, reducing the impact of successful attacks. Despite the high number of incidents, the lower financial damage indicates better preparedness and resilience within the crypto ecosystem.

For everyday users, this news highlights the improving security landscape in crypto. While the number of attacks remains high, the reduced financial impact means that individual users and smaller projects are less likely to suffer significant losses. This trend could encourage more people to participate in DeFi and other crypto activities with greater confidence.

Moving forward, users should continue to prioritize security best practices, such as using hardware wallets and enabling two-factor authentication. Keeping an eye on security reports and updates from platforms like Immunefi can also help users stay informed about potential risks and protective measures.

Despite more attacks, crypto losses are down. DeFi's improved security means less damage from exploits. Users can breathe a little easier.

To stay safe, use hardware wallets and 2FA. Follow security updates from Immunefi. Read more → https://cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-07-09-crypto-hack-losses-drop-below-1b-in-first-half-of-2026-despite-record-attacks
